response.justification: Candidate is a senior/staff-level engineer with 15+ years of experience applying for an entry-level Junior Java Developer role — a severe seniority mismatch that creates retention and compensation risk. While technical skills are strong and Java is listed, the actual hands-on Java experience is limited and dated (robotic arms work at Altom 2016-2018), with the core expertise being TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang rather than the Java/JVM ecosystem expected for insurance-sector enterprise work. German is only at basic level, which is a hard constraint for client-facing agile work with German insurance clients.
strengths: Extensive software craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, pairing, agile)
Broad polyglot experience including Java exposure and OOP fundamentals
Strong communication skills evidenced by workshops, talks, and teaching
concerns: Massive seniority mismatch — staff/senior engineer applying for junior role
German only at basic level, role requires strong written and verbal German
Java is peripheral to actual expertise (TypeScript/Node/DLang dominate recent work)
